title: Formula One's Push into America
subtitle: F1赛车如何打开美国市场

paragraphs.en: For the past four decades, the leader of Formula One car racing, one of the biggest annual sporting series in the world, was Bernie Ecclestone, a former motorcycle parts dealer who built it into an international presence essentially on his own. A skilled backroom operator who speaks without a filter, Ecclestone said often that in his opinion, the sport was at its best when he was allowed to act as "a dictator."
paragraphs.zh: 过去四十年间，全球最大年度体育赛事之一的一级方程式赛车掌门人一直是伯尼·埃克莱斯顿。这位前摩托车零部件经销商几乎是凭一己之力将F1打造成了国际盛事。作为一位说话毫不遮掩的娴熟幕后操盘手，埃克莱斯顿经常说，在他看来，只有让他像"独裁者"一样行事时，这项运动才处于最佳状态。

paragraphs.en: Yet now the dictator is gone. After an American company, Liberty Media, acquired the Formula One competition recently, Chase Carey — a former executive with Fox Broadcasting Company and DirecTV who by his own admission is not a fierce racing fan — was named to replace Ecclestone and to try to renovate the organization's management, reach and ambition.
paragraphs.zh: 然而现在，这位"独裁者"已经离开。在美国自由媒体集团近期收购一级方程式锦标赛之后，蔡斯·凯里——这位福克斯广播公司和DirecTV的前高管，据他自己承认并非狂热的赛车迷——被任命接替埃克莱斯顿，负责革新该组织的管理架构、影响力版图及雄心壮志。

paragraphs.en: Among the goals, Carey said in an interview on Tuesday, is one that just about every global sport seems interested in chasing: increasing interest in the United States. "People have said we're going to 'Americanize' it," Carey said. "And we're not going to do that totally. But realistically, there are some elements of Americanization that the sport could use."
paragraphs.zh: 凯里在周二的一次采访中表示，众多目标之一，正是几乎所有全球性体育项目都渴望追逐的：提升在美国的关注度。"有人说我们要把它'美国化'，"凯里说，"我们不会全盘照搬。但客观来讲，这项运动确实可以借鉴一些美国化的元素。"

paragraphs.en: While Formula One commands enormous audiences throughout much of the world, many American sports fans know it as that other motorsport, the one that is not Nascar. Formula One teams race far more technologically advanced vehicles around tracks all over the world — in magnificent events in places like Malaysia, Monaco, Singapore and the United Arab Emirates, and on tradition-rich tracks like Silverstone in England and Monza in Italy too.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管一级方程式赛车在全球大部分地区都拥有庞大的观众群，但许多美国体育迷只知道它是另一种赛车运动，一种不同于纳斯卡的赛事。F1车队驾驶着技术远为先进的赛车在全球各地赛道上角逐——既有在马来西亚、摩纳哥、新加坡和阿联酋等地举办的盛大赛事，也有在英国银石赛道和意大利蒙扎赛道这样传统深厚的场地上演的较量。

paragraphs.en: The series has an annual race in Austin, Texas. But within "a few years," Carey said, he plans to bring another to a destination American city, like New York, Los Angeles, Miami or Las Vegas. Carey's ambitious plan is two-fold: first, change the business model of Formula One, which he said was a "one-man show" under Ecclestone that had a largely narrow vision when it came to negotiating partnership deals; and second, alter the way fans experience the sport, both in person and remotely, so that connections between the audience and people within the series are easier to make.
paragraphs.zh: 该系列赛每年在得克萨斯州奥斯汀市有一站比赛。但凯里说，"几年内"他计划在美国的某个标志性城市再增设一站，比如纽约、洛杉矶、迈阿密或拉斯维加斯。凯里雄心勃勃的计划包含两方面：首先，改变F1的商业模式，他表示埃克莱斯顿治下的F1是"一人独揽大权"，在洽谈合作协议时视野相当狭窄；其次，改变车迷现场及远程观赛的体验，使观众与赛事相关人士之间的联系更加容易建立。

paragraphs.en: Increased digital access for fans, a more behind-the-scenes experience for broadcast viewers and innovation in areas like virtual reality — what is it like to speed around a track inside a Ferrari? — are among the possibilities. "The sport has clearly been underserved," Carey said. "It doesn't do anything digitally. There's no marketing. It doesn't tell any stories. The goal in this is to make the fans connect to the live experience as much as possible, and the tools you have to do that, we're not using at all."
paragraphs.zh: 为车迷提供更多数字化接入渠道、为电视观众呈现更多幕后花絮体验，以及在虚拟现实等领域的创新——比如，坐在法拉利车内飞驰赛道究竟是什么感觉？——都蕴含其中。"这项运动显然一直未得到充分开发，"凯里说，"它在数字化方面毫无作为，没有营销，也不讲任何故事。我们的目标是让车迷尽可能与现场体验产生共鸣，而我们手头那些本可用来实现这一点的工具，却完全没有派上用场。"

words.meaning: adj. 服务不周的；未充分照顾的

paragraphs.en: The larger question, though, is a familiar one: Is there room for Formula One in the ever-crowded sports landscape of the United States? Opinions vary, particularly because viewing habits among consumers continue to evolve. John Bloom, a professor at Shippensburg University who has studied American sports history, said the biggest challenge for any sport trying to increase its presence in the United States was framing itself in a way that had lasting appeal. "Sports generally become popular in some way because they establish a narrative," Bloom said. "When I think of motorsports in the U.S., what we all think of is Nascar, and the narrative of Nascar is sort of rural, white, working-class Americans, mostly in the South, connecting with the atmosphere of those races. That's the narrative. When I think of the narrative of Formula One, it's a very different kind of audience."
paragraphs.zh: 然而，更关键的问题还是那个老生常谈：在美国日益拥挤的体育版图中，F1还有立足之地吗？众说纷纭，尤其是消费者的观赛习惯还在不断演变。研究美国体育史的希彭斯堡大学教授约翰·布鲁姆认为，任何试图扩大在美影响力的体育项目面临的最大挑战，都是如何以一种具有持久吸引力的方式来构建自身的叙事。"体育运动之所以流行，通常是因为它们建立起了某种叙事，"布鲁姆说，"提起美国的赛车运动，我们都会想到纳斯卡，纳斯卡的叙事大体上围绕的是以南方为主的美国乡村、白人、工薪阶层，与他们与赛事氛围的共鸣。这就是它的叙事。而在我看来，F1的叙事所对应的观众群体则截然不同。"

paragraphs.en: That difference, Carey said, is significant. While some might immediately link Formula One to Nascar in terms of American growth, Carey said Formula One's brand research had indicated there was very little crossover; rather, Formula One fans generally cite other so-called elite events, like Wimbledon or the Ryder Cup, as competitions they enjoy. "Other than they're both cars, the Nascar fan base is a very different fan base," Carey said. "It's a very regional fan base. Formula One is a global, famous brand of stars. These are machines that shock and awe you."
paragraphs.zh: 凯里说，这种差异意义重大。尽管有人可能会立即将F1在美国的增长与纳斯卡联系起来，但凯里表示，F1的品牌研究表明两者之间几乎没有重叠；相反，F1车迷通常会提到温布尔登网球赛或莱德杯高尔夫球赛等其他所谓的精英赛事，作为他们喜爱的比赛。"除了都是赛车之外，纳斯卡的粉丝群体和我们的非常不同，"凯里说，"它的粉丝群地域性很强。而F1是一个全球性的、众星云集的知名品牌。这些赛车能让你感到震撼和敬畏。"

paragraphs.en: Carey's background is in deal making and innovation. At Fox Broadcasting Company, he was a top advisor for years, known for his skill in helping to lead the launch of company into sports, as well as the start of Fox News Channel. After going to DirecTV, he positioned the satellite provider as a mainstream option in millions of households.
paragraphs.zh: 凯里的背景是交易撮合与创新。在福克斯广播公司，他曾是多年的顶级顾问，以协助推动公司进军体育领域及创办福克斯新闻频道的才能而闻名。转投DirecTV后，他又将这家卫星电视提供商定位成了数百万家庭的主流选择。

paragraphs.en: Now, after Liberty Media paid $4.4 billion to acquire Formula One, he is charged with making the investment pay off. "I think they can build Formula One in the U.S.," said Patrick Crakes, an executive at InVivo Media Group who spent 25 years at Fox Broadcasting Company before leaving in 2016 as a senior manager at Fox Sports. "People don't work on their cars anymore. They don't want that connection anymore. It's about technology and pushing the limits. It's about speed, danger and risk. And Formula One has that more than any other racing series."
paragraphs.zh: 如今，在自由媒体集团斥资44亿美元收购F1之后，他肩负着让这笔投资产生回报的重任。InVivo媒体集团高管帕特里克·克雷克斯曾在福克斯广播公司任职25年，于2016年从福克斯体育高级经理的职位上离职。他说："我认为他们可以在美国成功打造F1。现在的人们不再自己动手修车了，也不再追求那种人车之间的联结感了。如今的关键词是科技和突破极限，是速度、危险与冒险。而F1在这些方面胜过任何其他赛车系列。"

words.meaning: phr. 取得成功；得到回报

paragraphs.en: That is what hooked Carey, and he said he thought his experience was not unusual. He recalled attending Formula One's Monaco race last year and being overwhelmed by the ceremony leading up to the event, the way the race charmed the city for days ahead of the start. In his mind it felt like a Super Bowl. Then, on race day, he watched as the cars rocketed out of a tunnel and went screaming toward a tight turn with the city's harbor and the Mediterranean Sea in the background framing the scene. He was fascinated. "You can't help but be awed," he said, "and I think that feeling can be translated to the viewer." He added: "The broader sport is a little too inward-looking, and we need to be more open. In some ways, I'm glad to be coming from the outside. The guys who are in the sport forever are sitting there saying: 'We can't do that. We can't do that because it's never been done that way.'"
paragraphs.zh: 这正是吸引凯里的地方，他说自己的这种体验应该不算特例。他回忆起去年参加F1摩纳哥大奖赛的经历，赛前那几天的仪式氛围、整座城市在开赛前数日被赛事所倾倒的景象，都令他深受震撼。在他的感受里，那就像是一场超级碗。待到比赛当天，他看着赛车从隧道中飞驰而出，尖叫着冲向一个急转弯，背景是城市的海港和地中海，画面美不胜收。他完全被迷住了。"你没法不感到敬畏，"他说，"而且我认为这种感觉是可以传递给观众的。"他还补充道："这项运动的大环境有点过于内向了，我们需要更加开放。从某些方面来说，我作为局外人来接手反倒是件好事。那些一辈子待在这项运动里的人只会坐在那里说：'我们不能这么做，从来没人这么做过。'"

words.meaning: adj. 入迷的；极感兴趣的
adj. 内向的；不关注外界的
